events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: commercial electrical, lighting controls & emergency egress, rough-complete, observed field condition: emergency fixture on switched leg. Reference NEC 700, NFPA 101. Determine compliance.

We need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Emergency fixture on switched leg means emergency lighting fixture is wired on a switched circuit (normal lighting switch controls it). NEC 700 requires emergency circuits be independent, no other loads, and emergency lighting must be automatically energized upon loss of normal power. If emergency fixture is on switched leg, switch could turn it off and it wouldn't illuminate during emergency. Also NFPA 101 requires emergency egress illumination. So non-compliant, fail. Risk high or critical? Emergency egress failure can be life safety critical. But rough-complete stage, can be corrected. Risk likely high or critical. The schema says high | critical. Since emergency egress non-compliance, critical maybe. But maybe high. Need decide. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete maybe 70? value 0. confidence 0.95. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. findings: emergency fixture wired on switched leg; switch can disable emergency egress lighting; violates independent emergency circuit requirement. actions: remove emergency fixture from switched leg; connect to unswitched emergency circuit; verify automatic transfer and operation. rationale. references: NEC 700.12 (general requirements), 700.16 (emergency illumination), NFPA 101 7.9 (emergency lighting). Need exact anchors maybe NEC 700.12(B), 700.16, NFPA 101 7.9.1/7.9.2.

Need output exactly one valid JSON object. No prose. Ensure all fields. Let's craft.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 70,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Emergency lighting fixture is wired on a switched normal-lighting leg.",
    "A wall switch can de-energize the emergency fixture, preventing automatic illumination during power failure or emergency.",
    "Emergency circuit is not independent and is subject to unauthorized or routine switching."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove the emergency fixture from the switched leg and connect it to a dedicated, unswitched emergency branch circuit.",
    "Verify the emergency circuit is supplied by an automatic transfer means that energizes upon normal power loss.",
    "Test fixture operation under normal and emergency power conditions before close-out."
  ],
  "rationale": "Emergency egress lighting must remain energized automatically during an emergency and cannot be controlled by a normal lighting switch. Wiring an emergency fixture on a switched leg allows the switch to disable a required life-safety device, violating NEC 700 and NFPA 101 emergency illumination requirements.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 700.12(B) - emergency circuits independent of general lighting",
    "NEC 700.16 - emergency illumination systems",
    "NFPA 101 7.9 - emergency lighting requirements"
  ]
}
